author: @reypolice5231
text:
Looks like a solution to what you described in video one. 

A cone at roof line that has a tail to make it face the wind. 

A 4-6 inch pipe down to ground to turn this design. 

I recommend that the end be tapered down to increase the force of the to the turbine intake with a 1-4 mm stand off to suck in ambient air.

Then a capacitor After the generator to take load off the 
turbine/generator.

Looks good for proof of concept. The bigger build will be great.

Thanks for the post.

author: @aliphian
text: lol at everyone saying "get rid of those 90 degree bends and you'll get a better result!" when the whole point is having the wind collected up high to run the turbine on the ground.

replies.author: @CurtisDrew1
replies.text:
In all my years of fabricating headers for my racing engines I have discovered that as long as the header tubes were the same size and all the bends were smooth on the outside radiuses, a bend had no real adverse effect on the air flow. As long as our headers were at approximatly 18 inches in length, they would scavange exhaust pulses very cleanly.  Mainly because engine exhaust travels in brusts from the cylinder exhaust port due to firing etc.
 I think that had he put a pointed cone inside the center of the fan to direct the air to the outside angled blades smoothly.  rather than against the flat center plate, it would have been much more effectient.  Plus had the venture been necked down at the fans input hole it would have inscreased the wind speed some as it was dispersed by the center cone.  And elliminated the overblast against the side of the fan blade. Air does not like to make flat cornereed turns!  But give it smooth outer walls to flow against and you will get much less turbulance.
But that's just my opinion.

replies.author: @davidoneill2466
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering The more i watch your videos….
The more you inspire…..
I have a young lad that we are building bicycle trailers. He builds and races off road race cars.  He built trailer #1 almost three years ago. It holds a 400wh battery and 200w of solar. And i rode it across America , California to New York.
I get up to 80km a day propulsion from the sun carting over 150lbs of camping gear.
Paul lives dab smack in the center of San Francisco.  I plan to be heading up there latter this month to continue working on Camper/Trailer #2.  It has 1000w motors in each wheel made by Grin from Canada. We are designing it to explore The Baja outback . I even have a small fridge/freezer to go into it…….
I am SUPER honored you responded……………

If you have radial turbine put air around the sides with some snail like housing. Air can get more speed before it hits that blades. Air can be directed with some little wings. Just like turbine in car.

Redirecting the wind certainly has a lot of merit and there are other ways  that come to mind well worth considering 
Roof ridges and corners of buildings for starters  or the plant room itself being the air concentrator 
Concentrating the air raising the temp would make heat pumps more efficient set after the turbine would help efficiency and could provide heated ventilation to boot  and first stage to air compression for second stage compression for  surplus electrical  get all that heat baby 
I really like this direction 😃
